name: Chicken & Chorizo Paella
steps: Cook rice with saffron and paprika per package directions.
Sear chicken and chorizo together 6-7 min.
Add bell pepper, cook 3 min more, combine with rice.
steps_detailed: Steep the saffron threads in 2 tablespoons of warm broth for 5-10 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a wide, shallow pan over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken and chorizo together, 5-6 minutes.
Add the bell pepper and garlic, cooking 2-3 minutes, then stir in the tomatoes and smoked paprika, simmering 3-4 minutes to build a sofrito base.
Stir in the rice, coating every grain in the sofrito, then add the remaining broth and the saffron broth.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook 18-20 minutes without stirring - resisting the urge to stir is what lets the bottom develop the prized crispy socarrat layer.
Let rest 5 minutes off heat before serving.
description: Paella depends on a specific short-to-medium-grain Spanish rice like Bomba, which absorbs liquid without turning mushy - basmati and jasmine are long-grain varieties built for an entirely different texture, and using either one here changes the dish's whole character, not just a minor detail.

name: Garlic Shrimp (Gambas al Ajillo)
steps: Saute garlic in olive oil until fragrant, 1 min.
Add shrimp and paprika, cook 2-3 min per side.
Serve with bread to soak up the oil.
steps_detailed: Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-low heat. Add the sliced garlic and cook slowly, 2-3 minutes, until fragrant and just starting to turn golden — don't let it brown, or it turns bitter.
Raise heat to medium-high, add the shrimp, and cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink.
Stir in the paprika off heat.
Serve immediately with bread for soaking up the garlicky oil, the classic tapas way.
description: Gambas al ajillo, shrimp sizzled in garlic-infused olive oil, is a classic Spanish tapas staple.

name: Spanish Chicken with Manchego
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Sear chicken 5-6 min per side, top with cheese to melt.
Serve with olives, tomatoes, and rice.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through (165F).
Add the tomatoes and olives in the last 2-3 minutes to warm through and soften slightly.
Top with shaved or crumbled manchego and serve over rice.
description: Manchego, Spain's most famous cheese, made from Manchega sheep's milk, paired here with chicken.

name: Spanish Tortilla (Potato & Egg)
steps: Saute potato and onion in olive oil until soft, 10-12 min.
Pour whisked eggs over, cook covered on low 8-10 min until set.
steps_detailed: Heat the olive oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-low heat. Add the potato and onion and cook slowly, 15-18 minutes, stirring occasionally, until very tender but not browned.
Whisk the eggs in a large bowl, then fold in the potato-onion mixture.
Wipe out the pan, add a little more oil if needed, and pour in the egg mixture over medium-low heat.
Cook 6-8 minutes until the edges set and the bottom is golden.
Place a large plate over the pan, flip the tortilla onto it, then slide it back into the pan to cook the other side, 4-5 more minutes.
Let rest a few minutes before slicing into wedges — it's traditionally served just warm or at room temperature, not piping hot.
description: Tortilla española is built on regular potato, not sweet potato - the mild, starchy flavor of a Yukon gold or russet-style potato is what actually belongs here, since sweet potato brings a completely different taste and texture to a dish that's supposed to taste primarily of egg, potato, and olive oil.

name: Chicken Paella
steps: Sear chicken, remove.
Toast rice with pepper and saffron, add broth and chicken back in, simmer covered 18-20 min.
steps_detailed: Steep the saffron in 2 tablespoons of warm broth.
Heat the olive oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat and brown the chicken, 5-6 minutes.
Add the bell pepper and garlic, cooking 2-3 minutes, then stir in the tomatoes and simmer 3-4 minutes to build a sofrito base.
Stir in the rice and saffron broth, then add the remaining broth.
Bring to a boil, reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook 18-20 minutes without stirring.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
description: Paella depends on Bomba or a similar short-to-medium-grain Spanish rice, not jasmine - jasmine is a long-grain variety built for an entirely different texture, and using it here changes the dish's whole character.

name: Spanish Garlic Shrimp
steps: Saute garlic in oil 1 min.
Add shrimp and paprika, cook 4-5 min until pink.
steps_detailed: Heat the olive oil over medium-low heat and cook the garlic slowly, 2 minutes, until fragrant but not browned.
Raise heat, add the shrimp, and cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink.
Stir in the paprika off heat before serving.
description: The same gambas al ajillo technique, garlic sizzled in olive oil with shrimp.

name: Spanish Beef and Potato Stew
steps: Sear beef until browned.
Add potato, tomatoes, and paprika, simmer covered 30-35 min until tender.
steps_detailed: Sear the beef in a pot over medium-high heat, 3-4 minutes, until browned. Remove.
Add the onion and garlic, cooking 3-4 minutes, then add the paprika, cooking 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes, potatoes, and beef back in, along with 1 cup water.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 35-40 minutes until the beef and potatoes are tender.
description: A simple, hearty Spanish beef and potato stew.

name: Spanish Baked Cod with Chorizo
steps: Saute chorizo 2 min, add tomatoes, simmer 5 min.
Nestle cod into the sauce, bake at 400F for 12-15 min.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F. Heat a splash of oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat and cook the chorizo 2-3 minutes to render some fat.
Add the garlic and tomatoes, simmering 8-10 minutes.
Nestle the cod into the sauce, spooning some over the top.
Bake 12-15 minutes until the fish flakes easily.
description: Cod and chorizo, a classic Spanish coastal pairing.

name: Spanish Chickpea and Spinach Stew
steps: Simmer chickpeas, tomatoes, and cumin 10 min.
Stir in spinach until wilted.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pot over medium heat. Add garlic, cumin, and paprika, cooking 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes and chickpeas, simmer 12-15 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in the spinach in the last 2 minutes until wilted.
description: Garbanzos con espinacas, a beloved Spanish tapas dish with roots in Sephardic Jewish and Moorish cooking.

name: Spanish Tuna and White Bean Jar
steps: Toss all ingredients together with oil.
steps_detailed: Whisk the olive oil and vinegar together.
Toss the beans and red onion with the dressing.
Layer in a jar with the tuna on top.
description: A simple, portable Spanish-style salad.

name: Spanish Chicken and Rice Jar
steps: Toss chicken with paprika.
Layer over rice with bell pepper.
steps_detailed: Toss the chicken with smoked paprika.
Layer the rice, bell pepper, and chicken in a jar.
description: A portable, paella-adjacent chicken and rice jar.

name: Spanish Egg and Potato Tortilla Wedge
steps: Saute potato and onion until soft.
Pour whisked eggs over, cook covered 6-8 min until set.
steps_detailed: Heat oil in a nonstick pan over medium-low heat. Cook the potato and onion slowly, 15 minutes, until tender.
Whisk the eggs, fold in the potato mixture, and pour back into the pan.
Cook 5-6 minutes until the edges set, then flip using a plate and cook 4 more minutes.
description: A smaller wedge of the classic tortilla española.

name: Spanish Baked Eggs (Huevos a la Flamenca)
steps: Simmer tomatoes and pepper 5 min in a small skillet.
Crack eggs into the sauce, cover and cook 5-6 min until set.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F. Heat a splash of oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Cook the onion and bell pepper 5-6 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and paprika, simmer 10 minutes.
Make wells in the sauce and crack the eggs into them.
Transfer to the oven and bake 8-10 minutes until the whites are set but yolks are still soft.
description: Huevos a la Flamenca, eggs baked in a spiced tomato and vegetable sauce, an Andalusian specialty.

name: Marcona Almond Snack
steps: Toss almonds with paprika.
steps_detailed: Toss the almonds with a light coating of olive oil and smoked paprika.
Toast in a dry skillet over medium-low heat, 3-4 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant.
description: Marcona almonds, a specific Spanish variety prized for their sweet, delicate flavor, a classic tapas snack.

name: Spanish Olives and Manchego Snack
steps: Serve cheese and olives together.
steps_detailed: Serve the manchego and olives together — a classic simple tapas pairing.
description: Olives and manchego, a classic Spanish tapas pairing.

name: Spanish Flan
steps: Melt sugar until amber, pour into ramekins.
Whisk eggs and milks, pour over caramel.
Bake in a water bath at 350F for 45 min, chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 350F.
Melt the sugar in a saucepan over medium heat, swirling (not stirring) until deep amber. Pour into ramekins, tilting to coat the bottom.
Whisk the eggs, condensed milk, evaporated milk, and vanilla together, then strain for smoothness.
Pour over the caramel. Place ramekins in a baking dish with hot water halfway up the sides.
Bake 40-45 minutes until just set with a slight jiggle.
Chill at least 3 hours, then run a knife around the edge and invert to unmold.
description: Flan, an egg custard with caramelized sugar, one of Spain's most beloved desserts.

name: Membrillo-Style Baked Pear
steps: Bake pear at 375F for 20 min.
Drizzle with honey and top with shaved manchego.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 375F.
Drizzle the pear halves with honey and place cut-side up on a baking sheet.
Bake 20-25 minutes until tender and caramelized at the edges.
Top with shaved manchego just before serving, a classic Spanish pairing of fruit and cheese.
description: Membrillo, quince paste, is traditionally paired with manchego - this version applies that same sweet-cheese pairing spirit to baked pear.

name: Romesco Sauce
steps: Blend all ingredients until mostly smooth.
steps_detailed: If roasting the pepper fresh, char it over a flame or under the broiler until blackened, then steam in a covered bowl 10 minutes and peel.
Toast the almonds in a dry skillet over medium heat, 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
Blend the roasted pepper, almonds, garlic, and vinegar until mostly smooth.
With the blender running, drizzle in the olive oil until the sauce comes together, slightly textured rather than perfectly smooth.
description: Romesco, a roasted red pepper and almond sauce from Catalonia, a defining Spanish condiment.

name: Spanish Alioli
steps: Whisk all ingredients together.
steps_detailed: Whisk all ingredients together. Let sit 10 minutes for the garlic to mellow slightly before serving.
description: Alioli, garlic emulsified into oil (traditionally without egg, unlike French aioli), a classic Spanish condiment.

name: Spanish Lentil Soup
steps: Simmer lentils, tomatoes, paprika, and onion with 1.5 cups water 25-30 min until tender.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pot over medium heat. Cook the onion 4-5 minutes, then add garlic and paprika, cooking 30 seconds.
Add the lentils, tomatoes, and 2 cups water. Bring to a simmer.
Cook 25-30 minutes until the lentils are tender.
description: A simple, hearty Spanish lentil soup.

name: Spanish Orange and Olive Salad
steps: Arrange orange segments, olives, and onion.
Drizzle with olive oil.
steps_detailed: Arrange the orange segments, olives, and onion on a plate.
Drizzle with olive oil just before serving.
description: Orange and olive, a refreshing combination found across southern Spanish cooking.

name: Gazpacho
steps: Blend all ingredients until smooth.
Chill at least 1 hour before serving.
steps_detailed: Blend all ingredients together until smooth.
Chill at least 1 hour — gazpacho is meant to be served very cold, and the flavors deepen as it sits.
description: Gazpacho, a cold, blended tomato soup from Andalusia, is meant to be eaten chilled, especially in summer heat.

name: Seafood Paella (Paella de Marisco)
steps: Steep the saffron in 1/4 cup of warm broth for 10 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a wide, shallow paella pan (or the widest skillet you have) over medium-high heat. Cook the bell pepper 3-4 minutes.
Add the garlic and paprika, cooking 30 seconds, then stir in the tomatoes and cook 3-4 minutes until slightly reduced.
Add the rice, stirring to coat every grain in the sofrito, then pour in the broth and saffron liquid.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and do not stir again — this is what lets the socarrat (the crisp, caramelized bottom layer) form.
Cook uncovered for 10 minutes, then nestle the shrimp and mussels into the rice.
Continue cooking 8-10 more minutes until the rice has absorbed the liquid, the shrimp are pink, and the mussels have opened (discard any that stay closed).
Remove from heat, cover loosely with foil, and let rest 5 minutes before serving.
steps_detailed: Steep the saffron threads in 1/4 cup of warm broth for at least 10 minutes — this is what gives paella its signature color and flavor, and pre-steeping matters more than dumping the threads straight into the pan.
Pat the shrimp and mussels dry. Scrub the mussel shells under cold water and pull off any visible beards. Discard any mussels that are cracked or don't close when tapped.
Heat the olive oil in the widest, shallowest pan you own over medium-high heat — surface area is what makes paella work, so a deep pot won't give the same result.
Add the onion and bell pepper, cooking 4-5 minutes until softened.

Add the garlic and paprika, cooking 30 seconds until fragrant, then stir in the tomatoes and simmer 3-4 minutes until slightly reduced — this is the sofrito, the flavor base for everything that follows.
Add the rice and stir for about a minute so every grain gets coated in the sofrito before any liquid goes in.
Pour in the broth and the saffron liquid, then spread the rice into an even layer across the pan.

Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer. From this point on, do not stir — resisting the urge to stir is the single most important technique in paella, since it's what allows the bottom layer to caramelize into socarrat instead of turning into risotto.
Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes, then nestle the shrimp and mussels into the rice, pushing them down slightly so they're partially submerged.
Continue cooking 8-10 more minutes, until the liquid is mostly absorbed, the shrimp are opaque and pink, and the mussels have opened — discard any mussels that remain closed after cooking.
In the last minute, listen and smell for a faint toasting aroma from the bottom — that's the socarrat forming. Pull it off heat right as you notice it, before it can actually burn.
Remove from heat, cover loosely with foil, and let rest 5 minutes. Serve directly from the pan with lemon wedges.
description: Paella de marisco, built on real Bomba rice, a proper sofrito base, and the patience to let socarrat - the prized crispy bottom layer - form without stirring, is the seafood version of Spain's most famous rice dish.
